content.problemStatement:
Chef has a **square-shaped** chart paper with the side length equal to NNN. He wants to cut out K×KK \times KK×K squares from this chart paper.

Find the **maximum** number of K×KK \times KK×K squares he can cut from the entire chart paper.

Note that, some part of the chart paper might not be a included in any K×KK \times KK×K cutout square.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT — the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first and only line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK — the side length of the entire chart paper and the side length of the cutout squares.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the **maximum** number of K×KK \times KK×K squares Chef can cut from the entire chart paper.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two space‑separated integers N and K — the side length of the square chart paper and the side length of the cutout squares.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the maximum number of K×K squares that can be cut from the N×N chart paper.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
For each test case we compute floor(N/K) and square it:
- N=5, K=1 → floor(5/1)=5 → 5^2=25
- N=2, K=2 → floor(2/2)=1 → 1^2=1
- N=5, K=2 → floor(5/2)=2 → 2^2=4

solution.approach: For a single test case we can place the small squares in a regular grid. Along one side of the big square we can fit floor(N / K) squares. The same number fits along the other side, so the total number of non‑overlapping K×K squares is (floor(N / K))^2. The computation is O(1) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work for each of the T test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it))
        K = int(next(it))
        cnt = N // K
        out.append(str(cnt * cnt))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
